author: @nitrousman8882
text: I think you might find that putting the roller and handle at right angles will give you greater force. (Ie Brayers)

author: @benniedonald
text: Brilliant job. I love watching your videos guys. I am running behind right now. Been a bit under the weather.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thank you so much! I hope you're feeling better, and hope you get well soon! :)

author: @LicensePlateHideGadget
text: don't act, just be you bud

author: @rayg436
text: if you don't have lock tight or a welder you can use a punch and hammer to put a dimple in the threads where the nut and rod end this will lock it in also.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Merry Holidays,  and happy new year! :)

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: excellent as always, got some rollers from laser printer cartridges that are made of aluminum I believe I want to try making some similar stuff :)

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That would be awesome! Them rollers are really nice, I think that is what I used in my first version of this roller. It worked perfectly. Thank you mate! :)

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey seems bigger units and copier units have these big thick rubber coated rollers too, Have two of different types of these that I am going to test out on a Van DeGraff generator, :)

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey something else I found in a larger printer was a unit that has a large open core as it had a long thin bulb for heating the roller up, and I wonder what all uses these can be used for, since the bearings are big enough to allow this while still being super fluid and easy to roll LOL

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text:
guessing you did not have a long enough piece of the flat bar to throw it into the bender and make it into a sort of flat bottomed U shape, with two holes on ends for mounting to the roller bar, and one in center to bolt your handle onto? seems like I have seen that design idea used on a brayer I had for rolling ink for block printing when I was much younger :) (something I think that laser engraver could be put to task to make either rubber stamps by engraving the rubber away, or a piece of that tile used for block printing where you carve what you want the ink to not land on and leave the paper inkless at, :) 

for Rob too, something that might be interesting to try would be that hot press sheeting that is metalized like thermal space blankets where its been vapor deposited with a mirror like coating of metal and then you heat it on something that when hot gets a bit sticky so it can pull the metal off and make a metal coating of sorts for things. (got some here that I have been using similarly for various tests and such)

author: @PetraKann
text:
1 million uses? 
I can only think of 27.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: 27 is a lot of uses! 1 million is an exaggeration because it does have a lot of uses for sure. :)

author: @chrissscottt
text: Nice one. A bit of bicycle inner tube might work to cover the roller too.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Awesome idea mate! Nice tight fit, with no need for any glue! :)

author: @totherarf
text:
Tip top top tip ...

.... if you'r out of locking agent and do not have a welder to hand (like most of us) Try cutting your threaded rod down a couple of mill proud of the nuts and tap the end with a hammer! 
Job Done .... Simples!

You don't have to waller it over, just a few taps will do. Especially if you have one of those strange hammers with a ball on one face that has no other use ;o)

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you, that'd be great for screen printing, amongst other things!!
Awesome, I have to make one of these, it will save me a lot of time and money. XxX

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Awesome! I really did like this one, I'm gonna do a bit of screen printing with it I think! Thank you! :)

author: @pedrotorres9836
text:
Cool
How thick of a sheet metal gauge will it straiten out?
If you use three of those together can you curve sheet metal?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: To be honest, I'm not sure as I haven't tried, I can imagine that it could straighten out a metal sheet (ali?) with the right amount of pressure. I will have to give it a go. Thanks mate! :)

author: @gyrgrls
text: If you used it on your cap,  could you make a roller derby?

replies.author: @michaelcorbidge7914
replies.text: His cap was on my mind . I found the curved peak caps can block a critical  section of driver's  vision when driving .

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: screen printing, lino printing, paper stick making, plus many more. I have used this for 2/3 of those ideas. Thanks mate. :)
